sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Needham sees AMD-Anthropic deal raising odds of more Riot Platforms leases
feed.summary: Investment bank Needham maintains a "Buy" rating and $28.50 price target for Riot Platforms following AMD's announcement of a deal with Anthropic. AMD's commitment to Anthropic, including using its AI chips and a potential $5 billion investment, is expected to increase the likelihood of AMD exercising its expansion rights at Riot's Rockdale data center or directing customers to Riot. Needham highlights Riot's Corsicana site as a significant asset for AI/HPC tenants, valuing its upside potential based on projected bitcoin prices and data center growth.

feed.title: SLM Q2 Earnings, Revenue Fall
feed.summary: SLM Corporation (Sallie Mae) reported a decline in its second-quarter earnings and revenue. The company's adjusted net income decreased to $0.80 per share, falling short of analyst expectations, primarily due to higher-than-anticipated charge-offs and a larger reduction in its managed loan portfolio. Despite these challenges, SLM reaffirmed its full-year earnings guidance, citing strong fundamentals in the student loan market and disciplined expense management.

feed.title: Citigroup (C) Announces Dividend Increase of 11.7%
feed.summary: Citigroup (C) has announced an 11.7% increase in its dividend payout to $0.67, signaling confidence in its financial health. While the company's GF Score™ of 74/100 indicates solid performance, particularly in growth (8/10), its financial strength is rated low (3/10), and insiders have shown net selling activity. Investors are advised to perform due diligence despite the positive dividend news.

feed.title: US business activity perks up in July, helped by World Cup, but trend may not last, S&P Global says
feed.summary: US business activity showed an unexpected acceleration in July, driven by increased services sector activity partly due to spending around the FIFA World Cup and Independence Day. However, S&P Global warns that this trend may be short-lived, as manufacturing growth eased and intensifying conflict in the Iran region is causing renewed supply chain delays and rising energy prices, posing risks to the economic outlook.

feed.title: Clorox stock trades steadily as margins recover and household demand supports guidance
feed.summary: Clorox's stock performance reflects a gradual recovery of margins following a cyberattack in late 2023, with recent results showing improved profitability and stable demand for its household brands. The company is focusing on cash generation, disciplined capital allocation, and maintaining its dividend, with its cleaning products anchoring a diversified portfolio. Its strong brand recognition and innovation in core categories help sustain market share and pricing power.

feed.title: Gold stuck in neutral as S&P Global highlights healthy US PMI activity
feed.summary: Gold prices are holding above $4,000 an ounce but lack sustained bullish momentum due to a resilient U.S. economy. S&P Global's flash Composite PMI reached an eight-month high in July, driven by strong services sector growth, while manufacturing also expanded despite a slight slowdown. This mixed economic data has had little immediate impact on the gold market.

feed.title: Stanley Black & Decker hit with class action over alleged tariff pricing scheme
feed.summary: Stanley Black & Decker is facing a class action lawsuit alleging that it unlawfully profited from tariffs imposed by the federal government. The lawsuit claims the company raised prices on Dewalt-branded products to offset tariff costs, and now stands to recover these tariff payments twice after the tariffs were ruled unauthorized. The plaintiff, Or Rothschild, seeks to represent a nationwide class of consumers for compensatory and punitive damages.

feed.title: Why Costco keeps gas prices so low—it's 'straight out of the retail playbook,' expert says
feed.summary: Costco maintains low gas prices as a strategic move to encourage members to visit warehouses more frequently, shop inside, and renew their memberships. This tactic has led to record demand for Costco's gasoline, with CEO Ron Vachris noting double-digit growth in gallons sold. Financial planner Lucas Bucl advises members to be aware of this retail strategy and create shopping lists to avoid impulse purchases that could negate fuel savings.

feed.title: Cardinal Health leans into CGMs, urology supplies
feed.summary: Cardinal Health is strategically expanding its presence in the continuous glucose monitors (CGMs) and urology supplies markets through key acquisitions, aiming for long-term growth. The company recently announced plans to acquire AdaptHealth's diabetes business for $235 million and Strive Medical for $125 million, building on a previous $1.1 billion acquisition of Advanced Diabetes Supply Group. These moves are designed to scale their offerings in product categories that align with their distribution model and patient experience focus, even as Medicare's competitive bidding program approaches for these supplies.

feed.title: GE HealthCare Q2 Revenue Rises 5.7% to $5.3B in Preliminary Results; CFO to Depart
feed.summary: GE HealthCare reported preliminary Q2 2026 revenue of $5.295 billion, a 5.7% year-over-year increase, with organic revenue growth of 3.5%. The company reaffirmed its full-year guidance and announced that CFO Jay Saccaro will depart on August 14, with George Newcomb named interim CFO. GEHC shares rose slightly following the announcement, suggesting investors were reassured by the reaffirmed guidance despite the CFO transition.

feed.title: Sanofi abandons eczema drug approval efforts in another blow to OX40 class
feed.summary: Sanofi has decided to discontinue the development of its immunology therapy, amlitelimab, for atopic dermatitis (AD), citing a lack of meaningful differentiation from existing treatments. This decision is a setback for Sanofi, which had hoped amlitelimab would succeed its blockbuster drug Dupixent, and further highlights challenges for the broader OX40 drug class, especially after concerns surfaced about Kaposi sarcoma in patients treated with a similar drug, rocatinlimab. Despite this, Sanofi will continue the Phase II study of amlitelimab for celiac disease, with results anticipated in the second half of 2026.

feed.title: Tesla Short Sellers Gain $9 Billion as Shares Tumble
feed.summary: Tesla Inc. stock recently dropped significantly after reporting weaker-than-expected second-quarter earnings, leading to substantial gains for short sellers. Short sellers pocketed $4.3 billion in mark-to-market gains from a single day's selloff, and their year-to-date unrealized gains reached $9.08 billion for 2026, making Tesla the most shorted stock among the Magnificent Seven. The company's high valuation and investments in future technologies like AI and robotics are raising questions about its growth prospects.

feed.title: Amgen submits new data to challenge FDA's proposal to withdraw Tavneos
feed.summary: Amgen has submitted new data to the FDA to challenge the proposed withdrawal of its rare-disease drug Tavneos from the U.S. market. The company argues that over 70 real-world studies involving more than 2,200 patients support the drug's effectiveness and safety, despite the FDA's earlier concerns regarding lack of proven effectiveness and false statements. This move comes after the FDA's April proposal to withdraw the drug and a recommendation from Europe's drug regulator to revoke its approval due to unreliable study data.

feed.title: Above the Noise: Look beyond AI pullback
feed.summary: Despite recent pullbacks in AI-related stocks, the author believes this is a recalibration of expectations rather than a fundamental breakdown, as strong earnings suggest the growth story is intact. A broader market rally is emerging, which historically supports longer market advances. Investors should also consider the potential implications of the policy outlook post-midterms on various market aspects like taxes and regulation.

feed.title: S&P Global July US Flash Manufacturing PMI 53.8 Vs. 54.4 Estimate; 53.9 Prior
feed.summary: S&P Global reported that the July US Flash Manufacturing PMI came in at 53.8, falling short of the estimated 54.4 and the prior figure of 53.9. This data was published on July 24, 2026, at 09:46 am EDT. The article presents the latest manufacturing PMI figures from S&P Global, indicating a slight dip compared to expectations and the previous month.

feed.title: Fifth Third (FITB) Stock Could Be Cheap On Value But Rich On Earnings
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bancorp's stock has seen a 124% return over three years, yet its valuation presents a mixed picture. While an Excess Returns model suggests the stock is 39.6% undervalued, traditional P/E ratios indicate it might be overvalued compared to industry averages and its fair P/E. The company's integration of Comerica and recent recognition as the U.S.'s Best Bank are positive factors, but the market also considers merger costs and risks associated with new AI initiatives.

feed.title: Fatal gene-editing trial in China raises ethics and oversight questions
feed.summary: A six-year-old girl has died in China after receiving an experimental, brain-directed CRISPR therapy, sparking significant ethical and oversight concerns. This tragic event was reported in conjunction with other biotech news, including an FDA panel narrowly voting to allow compounding pharmacies to produce certain unregulated peptides, a decision seen as a win for RFK Jr. The article also mentions Sanofi insulin pen shortages.

feed.title: SFGV ETF Profile: Dividends, Returns (BOATS:SFGV)
feed.summary: The SFGV ETF invests globally in equity securities, allocating 50-75% to US, 20-30% to other developed, and the rest to emerging markets, with allocations adjusting based on risk/return. It screens for positive shareholder yield and free cash flow yield, targeting attractively valued companies and strong-performing ETFs. The fund, issued by Empirical Finance LLC, has an expense ratio of 0.33%, a dividend yield of 2.35%, and its top holdings include iShares Core MSCI International Developed Markets ETF and Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets ETF.

feed.title: Corteva Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.18 a Share, Payable Sept. 15 to Holders of Record Sept. 1
feed.summary: Corteva, Inc. announced its quarterly dividend will remain at $0.18 per share. The dividend is payable on September 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of September 1, 2026. This information was officially released by MT Newswires.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks CEO warns OpenAI Hugging Face breach brings browser SASE to the fore
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks CEO Nikesh Arora commented on the recent Hugging Face breach, where OpenAI agents were involved, using the opportunity to promote Palo Alto's browser-based security solutions. Arora suggested that AI frontier labs should audit their internal infrastructure with AI models and deploy offensive and defensive AI agents to control autonomous models. He emphasized that the breach highlights the urgent need for enterprises, especially SMBs, to improve their security posture, advocating for secure browsers like Palo Alto's Prisma Access Browser.

feed.title: NextEra Energy (NEE) Q2 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: NextEra Energy reported strong Q2 2026 results with adjusted EPS growing 9.5% year-over-year to $1.15, and adjusted net earnings reaching $2,407 million. The company is positioned for long-term growth through strategic investments in renewables, storage, and transmission, and anticipates a merger with Dominion Energy to enhance shareholder value. NextEra Energy also provided an optimistic outlook, targeting over 8% adjusted EPS CAGR through 2032 and 10% dividend per share growth for 2026.

feed.title: Lamb Weston (LW) Q4 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: Lamb Weston announced its Q4 and fiscal year 2026 earnings, with net sales for the fiscal year rising 2% to $6,612 million, driven by strong North America performance. Adjusted EBITDA for the fiscal year reached $1,147 million, exceeding guidance, while net income was $290 million. The company provided fiscal 2027 guidance, projecting flat to 1% net sales growth, adjusted EBITDA of $1.1–$1.2 billion, and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.95–$3.25.

feed.title: Merck stock steadies as Keytruda drives Q1 2026 revenue growth
feed.summary: Merck & Co.'s stock is primarily driven by its oncology blockbuster Keytruda, which continues to propel revenue growth in Q1 2026. The company is investing billions in R&D to expand its pipeline beyond Keytruda, while also maintaining a significant cash return program. Keytruda remains a top-selling cancer medicine globally, and its performance, alongside pipeline developments and capital allocation, influences Merck's market valuation.

feed.title: Robin Energy prices $3 million public offering at $4 per share
feed.summary: Robin Energy Ltd. (NASDAQ:RBNE) announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 750,000 common shares at $4.00 per share, aiming to raise approximately $3.0 million in gross proceeds. The company, an international ship-owning entity providing energy transportation, intends to use the net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes. Maxim Group LLC is serving as the sole book-running manager for the offering, which is expected to close around July 27, 2026.

feed.title: Equity Residential suspends annual earnings outlook after merger agreement, quarterly FFO beats estimates
feed.summary: Equity Residential has suspended its full-year earnings outlook following its planned merger with AvalonBay Communities. The company reported adjusted FFO of USD 1.02 per share, exceeding analysts' estimates, and raised its full-year same-store net operating income growth guidance due to high occupancy and strong lease renewals. Despite higher interest rates, Equity Residential maintained positive operating performance in its key markets.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts Ameriprise Financial PT to $562 From $550, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has updated its price target for Ameriprise Financial (AMP) to $562, an increase from the previous $550, while retaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst revisions and recent earnings reports for Ameriprise Financial, which showed improved performance. The company specializes in wealth management, asset management, and insurance services.

feed.title: Raymond James Adjusts Price Target on American Electric Power to $145 From $144, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Raymond James has increased its price target for American Electric Power (AEP) from $144 to $145, reiterating an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook by the analyst firm for the utility company. The article also provides a brief company profile for American Electric Power, detailing its primary activities in electricity production, transport, distribution, and transmission facility operations.

feed.title: GE HealthCare CFO Jay Saccaro to Step Down
feed.summary: GE HealthCare announced that Executive VP and CFO James “Jay” Saccaro will step down on August 14th to pursue an opportunity outside the medical technology industry. George Newcomb, the company's controller and chief accounting officer, has been named interim CFO while a permanent successor is sought. The company also released preliminary second-quarter 2026 results, expecting revenue and organic revenue growth, and reaffirmed its full-year outlook.

feed.title: Red River Bancshares (RRBI) investor Thibeaux reports 200,000-share, 3% ownership via trusts
feed.summary: Simeon A. Thibeaux has filed an updated ownership report for Red River Bancshares Inc. (RRBI), disclosing a beneficial ownership of 200,000 shares, which represents 3.0% of the common stock. These shares are held through two trusts, the John Charles Simpson Jr. Trust and the Angela Katherine Simpson Trust, for both of which Thibeaux serves as the sole trustee, giving him sole voting and dispositive power. The filing was made as a Schedule 13G/A, indicating passive investment under 5% ownership.

feed.title: Pest controller Rentokil challenged by slowing US growth - says analyst
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank analysts predict that Rentokil Initial PLC will face challenges in North America due to slowing industry growth in pest control. They expect North American Pest Control organic growth to decline to 3% in Q2 from 4.1% in Q1, influenced by weaker online demand for one-off pest treatments. Despite this, Deutsche Bank forecasts first-half revenue of $3.53 billion and EBIT of $562 million, maintaining a "Hold" rating with a 465p price target.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Price Target on O'Reilly Automotive to $100 From $110, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has lowered its price target for O'Reilly Automotive (ORLY) from $110 to $100, while maintaining an "Overweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised outlook from the analyst firm. The article also notes other recent analyst actions and company news concerning O'Reilly Automotive.

feed.title: Bean Size Concerns Emerge as Brazil's Coffee Harvest Accelerates
feed.summary: Brazil's coffee harvest is accelerating, but concerns are emerging regarding bean size, particularly in key arabica regions like South Minas and Mogiana Paulista. While volume expectations remain high, indicating a potential record crop, a larger proportion of beans falling into smaller screen categories could impact export premiums and roaster preferences. This development suggests that while overall supply might be abundant, quality differentials and pricing strategies could become more complex, especially for specialty buyers.

feed.title: Arabica Futures Extend Recovery on Weather Risks and Tight Inventories
feed.summary: Arabica coffee futures have extended their recovery due to renewed weather disruptions in Brazil and declining certified inventories, despite concerns about a rapidly improving global supply outlook. The market is experiencing volatility as Brazil's harvest progresses, caught between immediate supply risks and bearish longer-term production forecasts. While a record crop is anticipated for 2026/27, current low certified arabica stocks and potential El Niño impacts on future flowering cycles add complexity.

feed.title: Mizuho Adjusts Price Target on Travelers Companies to $343 From $324, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Mizuho has adjusted its price target for Travelers Companies (TRV) to $343, up from $324, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This update reflects a re-evaluation of the company's financial outlook by the analyst firm. The article also lists other recent analyst recommendations for Travelers Companies, with various price target adjustments and ratings.

feed.title: Analysts Are Bullish on Top Industrial Goods Stocks: United Airlines Holdings (UAL), Waste Connections (WCN)
feed.summary: Two analysts have issued bullish sentiments on top industrial goods stocks: United Airlines Holdings (UAL) and Waste Connections (WCN). Wells Fargo maintained a Buy rating on UAL with a $165.00 price target, while Truist Financial maintained a Buy rating on WCN. Both companies have an analyst consensus of Strong Buy.

feed.title: Selective pulls back on premiums as casualty pressure builds
feed.summary: Selective Insurance Group experienced an accelerated premium decline in Q2 2026, with net premiums written falling 5% as the insurer reduced new business and retained fewer poor-performing accounts. Despite this decline, the company returned to an underwriting profit, with its combined ratio improving to 98% due to lower property losses and an absence of adverse prior-year casualty reserve development, even as current-year casualty loss costs increased significantly. Selective maintained its full-year combined-ratio forecast and updated its investment income outlook.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Price Target on Extra Space Storage to $158 From $154, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has increased its price target for Extra Space Storage Inc. (EXR) to $158 from the previous $154, while upholding its "Overweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook from the analyst firm. Extra Space Storage specializes in owning and managing self-storage facilities and has a real estate portfolio of 4,281 assets across the United States.

feed.title: Newmont Resources delivers mixed second quarter earnings
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation reported mixed financial results for the second quarter, with revenue slightly missing analyst expectations despite achieving record free cash flow. The company's adjusted earnings per share exceeded consensus, and it reaffirmed its full-year gold production guidance of 5.3 million attributable ounces. Newmont also secured key regulatory approvals for its Red Chris Block Cave project, advancing it towards a final investment decision.

feed.title: These Analysts Increase Their Forecasts On Union Pacific Following Strong Q2 Results
feed.summary: Union Pacific (UNP) reported stronger-than-expected Q2 2026 results with adjusted diluted EPS of $3.41 and operating revenue of $6.864 billion, both surpassing estimates. Following these positive results, Wells Fargo and Benchmark analysts maintained their positive ratings and raised their price targets for the stock. Management also indicated that second-half demand is tracking above initial expectations.

feed.title: SL Green Realty Corp. Revenue Breakdown – LSE:0KZ6
feed.summary: SL Green Realty Corp. (LSE:0KZ6) generated $601.54 million in total revenue last year. Its top-performing segment, Real Estate, contributed $788.59 million, an increase from $709.73 million the previous year. The United States was the primary regional contributor, accounting for $1.00 billion in revenue.

feed.title: Bank of America hikes common stock dividend by 14% By Reuters
feed.summary: Bank of America has announced a 14% increase in its quarterly common stock dividend, raising it by 4 cents to 32 cents per share. This decision aligns with the bank's commitment to returning excess capital to shareholders, following strong second-quarter profits driven by global market activity and a surge in corporate dealmaking. Other banks have also increased dividends and initiated share buyback programs after recent Federal Reserve stress test results.

feed.title: SLB reports international growth as offshore activity offsets Middle East disruptions
feed.summary: SLB announced higher second-quarter revenue of $8.97 billion, driven by broad-based international growth, particularly in offshore activities. This growth successfully offset ongoing disruptions in the Middle East. The company saw strong performance in Production Systems and Digital revenue, with offshore developments in Latin America, Europe & Africa, and Asia contributing significantly to these gains.

feed.title: AI token routing cuts enterprise cloud costs with AMD
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. is advocating for AI token routing as a critical strategy to manage the escalating costs associated with enterprise AI deployments. According to AMD's John Hampton, companies are moving beyond initial AI experimentation and discovering that reactive infrastructure choices lead to exorbitant "token bills." AMD itself implemented this strategy internally, achieving a 43% reduction in token costs and a 2.9x increase in response speed by optimizing workload routing to more efficient hardware, such as their MI350P GPUs, instead of exclusively relying on expensive cloud-based frontier models.

feed.title: American Express lifts revenue forecast as card spending jumps 9%
feed.summary: American Express (AXP-N) exceeded second-quarter profit forecasts and increased its 2026 revenue projections, driven by a 9% rise in card spending from affluent customers. Despite the positive performance, the company's shares declined as investors focused on its unchanged full-year profit outlook. American Express plans to reinvest its overperformance into marketing to accelerate growth, especially targeting Gen Z and millennial customers.

feed.title: SHG|Shinhan Financial Group Co Ltd|Price:75.000|Chg%:+3.350
feed.summary: Shinhan Financial Group Co Ltd (SHG) shows stable fundamentals, high growth potential, and is considered fairly valued within the banking services industry. Institutional ownership is high, and analysts rate it as a Strong Buy with a target price of 97.29. The stock has shown strong performance over the past month, but technical indicators suggest sideways trading in the short term.

feed.title: Surgery Partners Announces Agreement to Sell Idaho Falls Hospital Interests to Intermountain Health for $795 Million
feed.summary: Surgery Partners has announced its agreement to sell its ownership interests in Mountain View Hospital and Idaho Falls Community Hospital to Intermountain Health for approximately $795 million. This transaction is part of the company's portfolio optimization strategy, which aims for a sharpened focus on the ambulatory surgery center market. The sale, which values the combined facilities at about $1.15 billion, is subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, with physician ownership of Mountain View Hospital remaining unchanged.

feed.title: Mapfre to Acquire Safety Insurance Group for $1.54 Billion in All-Cash Deal
feed.summary: Mapfre, a global insurer, is set to acquire Safety Insurance Group for $1.54 billion in an all-cash deal, offering Safety shareholders a 44% premium. This acquisition will strengthen Mapfre's presence in the New England property and casualty insurance market. Safety will operate as a wholly-owned subsidiary, retaining its brand and management team, benefiting from Mapfre's financial resources and expertise.

feed.title: Micron, SanDisk Stock Rout Is Ugly — This New ETF Thinks That’s Perfect - Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU)
feed.summary: A new ETF, T-REX 2X Inverse DRAM Daily Target ETF (RAMZ), is launching on July 28 to allow traders to profit from or hedge against declines in AI memory semiconductors. This launch comes as memory chip stocks like Micron Technology and SanDisk Corp have seen a selloff after an earlier AI-driven rally. T-REX already offers leveraged bullish products for this sector, and RAMZ will complete the ecosystem by providing a bearish option, reflecting the evolving tools for navigating market volatility.

feed.title: BlackRock Kicks Off $12.3 Billion Bond Sale for Meta Data Center
feed.summary: BlackRock has launched a $12.3 billion high-grade bond sale to finance a massive Meta Platforms data center project in El Paso, Texas. This move tests investor interest in AI infrastructure spending and comes amidst concerns about excessive investment in the sector. BlackRock subsidiaries and HPS Investment Partners own an 80% stake in the project, with Meta holding the remaining 20%.

feed.title: Guggenheim cuts Six Flags stock price target on softer attendance By Investing.com
feed.summary: Guggenheim has lowered its price target for Six Flags Entertainment (NYSE:FUN) from $33 to $28, citing weaker second-quarter attendance trends and a "WEAK" financial health score. The firm revised its Q2 attendance and revenue forecasts downwards due to these factors and external influences like a hot July start and Canadian wildfires. Despite the stock's significant decline, management is focusing on optimizing its asset portfolio, reducing debt, and enhancing the guest experience.
